exemplar.title: Can new knowledge change established values or beliefs?

detailedExemplarData.title: Can new knowledge change established values or beliefs?

strengths: Clear identification of three specific, real-world objects with well-defined contexts
Coherent links made between each object and IA prompt 11, showing how new knowledge changed established beliefs
Use of concrete historical and contemporary evidence (e.g., Vatican ban lift, ITER collaboration, Brocaâs patient) to support justifications
weaknesses: Analysis sometimes repetitive and lacking fresh evidence in the conclusion
Several assertions (e.g. internet misinformation, wartime neuroscience acceleration) are not well supported or precisely sourced
Language and syntax occasionally complex or imprecise, hindering clarity and depth
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The commentary clearly meets the 7â8 band descriptors: three concrete objects are identified in their real-world contexts, and links to the chosen TOK prompt are generally coherent and focused. The student supports most points with appropriate evidence and references, offering persuasive justifications for each objectâs contribution. However, the analysis falls short of the top bandâs requirement for nuance and precision: some claims lack robust sourcing, phrasing is at times repetitive or unclear, and scholarly references are unevenly integrated. These limitations justify a solid score of 7 within the 7â8 band, reflecting strong performance with room for deeper analysis and tighter argumentation.

annotations.comment: The clear statement of IA Prompt 11 sets a focused direction for the exhibition, ensuring the studentâs inquiry is well defined from the start.
The student should introduce a brief framing paragraph explaining why this prompt was chosen, enhancing the coherence between introduction and objects.
Consider defining key TAK terms such as âvaluesâ and âbeliefsâ here to help the reader understand how they connect to new knowledge.
The section title âTOK Exhibitionâ is concise and effectively signals the task, giving the document a clear academic structure.
The introduction lacks an explicit statement of how the chosen prompt relates to upcoming objects; adding a transitional sentence would improve flow.
Identifying Copernicusâs exact copy of De Revolutionibus grounds the object in a specific real-world artifact, strengthening contextual clarity.
The student asserts the Churchâs condemnation due to âstubborn valuesâ without citing specific ecclesiastical records; add precise historical sources.
Provide clearer evidence for the 1757/1758 ban lift, such as a citation of the Vatican decree, to substantiate this pivotal date.
The commentary describes technological outcomes (telescopes, scientific method) but stops short of explicitly linking these to changed beliefs; clarify the TOK connection.
Cite usage statistics or a study to support the claim of dramatic change in research beliefs post-internet, strengthening the evidence base.
The ITER example illustrates global collaboration well, but the link to belief change could be sharpened by discussing scientific consensus evolution.
The statement about decreased objectivity due to misinformation is important but unsupported; include a case study or statistic.
Avoid labeling individuals as ânaÃ¯ve peopleâ; use precise terminology (e.g., âless experienced information consumersâ).
The final paragraph repeats the idea of resistance to new knowledge but lacks fresh evidence; consider condensing to avoid redundancy.
Could the student explain more precisely how heliocentrismâs acceptance shifted the value placed on scientific authority versus religious tradition?
Linking the home router to the TOK theme of technology effectively contextualizes a mundane object in a knowledge framework.
The generic definition of the internet offers little TOK insight; integrate this into a discussion of how access to information alters belief systems.
Selecting Brocaâs patient âTanâ as an object introduces a tangible case of new knowledge challenging neurological beliefs.
The definition of brain localization contains misplaced parentheses and lacks precision; revise for clarity and accuracy.
Support the claim that world wars accelerated neuroscience with a historical study or publication to avoid anecdotal reasoning.
Provide precise dates for Brocaâs discovery (e.g., 1861) to anchor the narrative in a clear historical timeline.
The closing argument links brain localization to PTSD treatment effectively but could explicitly state how beliefs about mental illness changed.
What specific online misinformation events could you analyze to show values shifting in response to falsehoods?
Several sentences contain complex syntax that impedes clarity; simplify language to ensure the argument remains accessible.
Could the student add a brief conclusion summarizing how all three objects collectively illustrate the promptâs theme?

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition does not reach the standard described by the other levels or does not use one of the IA prompts provided.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of the objects may be implied rather than explicitly stated. Basic links between the objects and the selected IA prompt are made, but the explanation of these links is unconvincing and/or unfocused. There is a superficial justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Reasons for the inclusion of the objects are offered, but these are not supported by appropriate evidence and/or lack relevance to the selected IA prompt. There may be significant repetition across the justifications of the different objects

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of these objects may be vaguely or imprecisely stated. There is some explanation of the links between the three objects and the selected IA prompt. There is some justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Some of the points are supported by evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects and their real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are explained, although this explanation may lack precision and clarity in parts. There is a justification of the contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. Many of the points are supported by appropriate evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition clearly identifies three objects and their specific real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are clearly made and well-explained. There is a strong justification of the particular contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. All, or nearly all, of the points are well-supported by appropriate evidence and explicit references to the selected IA prompt.
